title: Can receive e-mail but can't send
creator: business3
content:
Just installed a new hard drive and windows 11. Installed Thunderbird, which I have used for years. Began receiving e-mail right away. Have tried to send several e-mails but I get an error message saying mail server responded: relay access denied Please check message recipient and try again. The e-mail for the recipient is correct and I have tried to send to others with the same result.

Do not know if it is a windows error, an smtp error or a Thunderbird error. My web host gave me the proper settings for incoming and out going mail. They are the same as I had for windows 10 which worked forever.

Can you help? Thanking you in advance.

title: Import more outlook accounts into the local folder in thunderbird
creator: jan.domney
content:
I had 3 different email accounts operating in outlook originally under a pop3 format. I would like to have all those email show up in the local folder with all the subfolders so I can save the imap emails currently coming in to thunderbird into the local folder and subfolders if I want to keep them. I have managed to download the email account that was the default in outlook through the import function. I have tried the import export tools and the local folders add ons but dont seem to be able to pick up the other email folders in the outlook file. Am I doing something incorrectly or, if I open outlook again could I change the default and then thunderbird would pick up and add the default through the import function.

Suggestions or corrections to my assumptions would be wonderful.

title: Import does not work
creator: austoniac
content:
The import function does not work. I exported a Thunderbird profile in its entirety into a zip file that is only 127 megabytes when extracted. When I go to import the Thunderbird profile, I select the ZIP file, import, and it restarts, and nothing happens.

I have tried the method of going to the profile folder, manually copying the files from the extracted profile, and pasting them into the profile folder and overwriting all of the existing files, which will work for a few seconds- it briefly shows all the emails, and then they disappear. The top of the inbox indicates the correct number of emails, but no emails show up in the inbox, and when I restart Thunderbird, everything disappears.
